resolves: review_v1_architecture.md C1, H1, H2, M1, M2
review_v1_completeness.md COMP-C-001 (rejected), COMP-C-002, COMP-C-003 (rejected), COMP-H-001, COMP-H-002, COMP-H-003
review_v1_consistency.md C-001, C-002, C-003, C-004, C-005, C-006, C-007

primary_summary: Add an archive lifecycle to Mealie's ShoppingList entity: extend with archived_at + archived_by_user_id columns (FK ON DELETE SET NULL); new POST /archive and /unarchive endpoints; default GET filters archived lists out; ?archived=true|all reveals them; archived lists become immutable for EVERY list-mutating route (PUT list / POST item / PUT item / DELETE item / PUT label-settings / POST recipe / DELETE recipe all return 409 with i18n key shopping-list.archived.frozen); defense in depth uses repository-layer guard PLUS service-layer pre-flight for the routes that bypass the repo guard; HTTP/i18n translation lives in a global FastAPI exception handler in mealie/routes/handlers.py (services stay HTTP-free); emit ShoppingListArchived/Unarchived events ONLY on actual state transition (unarchive on already-active list is a no-op without event); event payload uses input's exact field names list_id/list_name; multitenant isolation via existing HouseholdRepositoryGeneric scoping plus tenant-scoped WHERE clauses on archive/unarchive writes; centralised in mealie/repos/repository_shopping_list.py per input §7.

selected_approach.name: Soft delete + repository-level frozen guard + service-level pre-flight (Group B)
selected_approach.rationale: Selected per input §7 mandate 'centralised in repository_shopping_list.py'. Places default-exclude filter AND frozen-state guard in the repository layer for all routes that write through RepositoryShoppingList.update or RepositoryShoppingListItem.{create,update,delete}_many. Routes that bypass these methods (label-settings via shopping_list_multi_purpose_labels.update_many; recipe-add/remove via add_recipe_ingredients_to_list) get an explicit service-layer pre-flight check (ensure_list_not_archived) before any write. Repos raise typed Python exception ShoppingListIsArchivedError (no HTTP/i18n concerns); a global FastAPI exception handler in mealie/routes/handlers.py translates to HTTPException(409, ErrorResponse.respond(message=translator.t('shopping-list.archived.frozen'))). Existing RepositoryShoppingList.update override (lines 9-11) is the natural seam, expanded from passthrough into guarded passthrough.
candidates_considered: A. Soft delete (filter only, no guard) — rejected: violates input §3.
B. Soft delete + service-level guard — strong alternative but does not match input §7's 'centralised' wording as literally as Option C.
C. Soft delete + repository-level guard + service-level pre-flight — SELECTED (v2 adds the service-level pre-flight to close the Group B gap).

user_stories.title: Archive a shopping list (success path)
user_stories.i_want: to mark a fully-completed shopping list as archived
user_stories.so_that: the main shopping-list view stays uncluttered and I retain historical records
user_stories.acceptance: Given I am a member of household H1 and ShoppingList L1 has household_id == H1 and all items checked, when I POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{L1.id}/archive, then response is 200 OK with ShoppingListOut where archived_at is the current UTC timestamp and archived_by is my UserSummary; the row is persisted with archived_at IS NOT NULL and archived_by_user_id = me.id; one EventTypes.shopping_list_archived event is dispatched with EventShoppingListArchiveData payload.

user_stories.title: Archive blocked when unchecked items present
user_stories.i_want: the system to refuse to archive a list that still has unchecked items
user_stories.so_that: I don't accidentally lose track of in-progress purchases
user_stories.acceptance: Given ShoppingList L1 has any item with checked == None or checked == False, when I POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{L1.id}/archive, then response is 409 CONFLICT with detail.message == 'Cannot archive a shopping list while items remain unchecked' (en-US value of shopping-list.archive.unchecked-items); the row is unchanged; no event is dispatched.

user_stories.title: Default GET filters archived lists out
user_stories.i_want: the default shopping-lists view to show only active lists
user_stories.so_that: I see a clean current working set
user_stories.acceptance: Given household H1 contains L_active and L_archived, when I GET /api/households/shopping/lists, then response items contains L_active.id and not L_archived.id; total reflects active-only count.

user_stories.title: ?archived=true and ?archived=all reveal archive history
user_stories.i_want: explicit query parameters to view archived-only or all lists
user_stories.so_that: I can review history without polluting the default view
user_stories.acceptance: GET /api/households/shopping/lists?archived=true returns only archived lists with archived_at + archived_by populated; ?archived=all returns both active (archived_at: null, archived_by: null) and archived lists.

user_stories.title: Frozen-state: every list-mutating route returns 409
user_stories.i_want: every mutating operation on an archived list (or its items, labels, or recipe references) to be rejected with a clear frozen error
user_stories.so_that: archived history is preserved as a true historical record
user_stories.acceptance: Given ShoppingList L1 has archived_at IS NOT NULL, when I attempt any of: PUT /lists/{L1.id}, POST /items (targeting L1), POST /items/create-bulk, PUT /items/{I.id}, PUT /items (bulk), DELETE /items/{I.id}, DELETE /items (bulk), PUT /lists/{L1.id}/label-settings, POST /lists/{L1.id}/recipe, POST /lists/{L1.id}/recipe/{rid} (deprecated), POST /lists/{L1.id}/recipe/{rid}/delete, then each response is 409 CONFLICT with detail.message == 'This shopping list is archived and cannot be modified. Unarchive it first.' (en-US value of shopping-list.archived.frozen); no rows mutated in shopping_lists / shopping_list_items / shopping_list_multi_purpose_label_settings / shopping_list_recipe_references; updated_at unchanged. Atomic pre-flight semantics: the service-level pre-flight on Group B routes runs BEFORE any sub-call to bulk_create_items / update_many.

user_stories.title: Unarchive restores mutable state
user_stories.i_want: to unarchive a list back to mutable state
user_stories.so_that: I can correct mistakes or repurpose an archived list
user_stories.acceptance: Given ShoppingList L1 has archived_at IS NOT NULL, when I POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{L1.id}/unarchive, then response is 200 OK with archived_at == null and archived_by == null; the row has archived_at IS NULL and archived_by_user_id IS NULL; L1 reappears in default GET /lists; subsequent PUT /lists, POST /items, PUT /items, DELETE /items, PUT /label-settings, and recipe routes succeed; exactly one EventTypes.shopping_list_unarchived event is dispatched (because a state transition occurred).

user_stories.title: Event bus dispatches with correct payload (no cross-household leak)
user_stories.as_a: webhook/notifier subscriber configured for shopping-list-archive events in my household
user_stories.i_want: to receive exactly the spec'd payload fields on archive/unarchive
user_stories.so_that: I can integrate downstream systems without leaking other tenants' data
user_stories.acceptance: Subscribed via GroupEventNotifier with shopping_list_archived = true for H1; when any user in H1 archives a list, exactly one Event arrives with event_type == EventTypes.shopping_list_archived and document_data of type EventShoppingListArchiveData whose model_dump().keys() equals exactly {'document_type', 'operation', 'list_id', 'list_name', 'household_id', 'archived_by_user_id', 'item_count', 'total_estimated_amount'} (first two inherited from EventDocumentDataBase per event_types.py:88-91; rest declared on EventShoppingListArchiveData); operation == EventOperation.update; payload household_id equals H1; no Event arrives at subscribers in other households; symmetric for shopping_list_unarchived.

user_stories.title: Multitenant isolation
user_stories.as_a: member of household H1 in group G1
user_stories.i_want: archived shopping lists to remain invisible to other households and groups
user_stories.acceptance: Given H1 owns archived L1; H2 (same group G1) owns archived L2; H3 (group G2) owns archived L3; when I (in H1) GET /api/households/shopping/lists?archived=true, items contains only L1.id; when I POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{L2.id}/archive OR /unarchive, response is 404 NOT FOUND; when I POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{L3.id}/archive OR /unarchive, response is 404; when a user in H3 (group G2) issues GET /api/households/shopping/lists?archived=true, response does NOT include L1.id or L2.id; symmetric for all six (caller-household, target-household) pairs.

user_stories.title: Schema backward compatibility (null-default, not field-omit)
user_stories.as_a: existing API consumer that does not read archived_at/archived_by
user_stories.i_want: the existing ShoppingListOut / ShoppingListSummary shapes to remain backward-compatible
user_stories.so_that: my pre-feature code continues to work without changes
user_stories.acceptance: Default GET response items have archived_at: null and archived_by: null which existing consumers ignore (JSON null on unknown field is RFC 8259 forward-compatible); all existing tests in test_group_shopping_lists.py (1113 lines, 7 baseline test methods) continue to pass unchanged. Input §6 'default query does not return these fields' is satisfied at the request-filtering layer (collection endpoint default-hides archived ROWS entirely) rather than field-projection — see exploration/consolidated.md CRITICAL-3.

user_stories.title: Scheduled cleanup respects frozen state
user_stories.so_that: archived history is preserved against automatic trimming
user_stories.acceptance: When the scheduled task runs against a household with both active and archived lists having >100 checked items each, active list is trimmed (existing behavior); archived list left fully intact; no ShoppingListIsArchivedError raised from scheduler context.

functional_requirements.title: Data model — ShoppingList extension
must_haves: Add archived_at: FilterableColumn[datetime | None] = mapped_column(NaiveDateTime, index=True) — default NULL = active

Add archived_by_user_id: FilterableColumn[GUID | None] = mapped_column(GUID, ForeignKey('users.id', ondelete='SET NULL'), index=True) — default NULL; ondelete='SET NULL' MUST be explicit at the model layer to match the migration in FR-2
Add archived_by: Mapped[Optional['User']] = orm.relationship('User', foreign_keys=[archived_by_user_id]) — disambiguating against the existing user relationship at line 156
Update existing user relationship at line 156 to specify foreign_keys=[user_id] for SQLAlchemy disambiguation

Invariant (ONE-WAY): archived_at IS NULL ⇒ archived_by_user_id IS NULL. Enforced at service layer on archive/unarchive transitions. The REVERSE direction does NOT hold: an archived row MAY have archived_by_user_id IS NULL after the archiving user is deleted (FK is ON DELETE SET NULL). This loosened invariant resolves the contradiction with EC-8/NC-5 (review_v1_consistency C-001, review_v1_completeness COMP-H-002).

functional_requirements.title: New API endpoints — archive / unarchive
must_haves: POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{item_id}/archive -> archive_one(self, item_id: UUID4) -> ShoppingListOut
POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{item_id}/unarchive -> unarchive_one(self, item_id: UUID4) -> ShoppingListOut
Decorators include response_model=ShoppingListOut, status_code=200, responses={409: {'model': ErrorResponse}, 404: {'model': ErrorResponse}}
Auth inherited from BaseCrudController (no extra Depends) — any household member
Each delegates to result = self.service.archive_list(item_id, self.user.id) / result = self.service.unarchive_list(item_id) where result is ArchiveTransitionResult(shopping_list, transitioned)

Dispatch EventTypes.shopping_list_archived / EventTypes.shopping_list_unarchived via self.publish_event(..., group_id=result.shopping_list.group_id, household_id=result.shopping_list.household_id, ...) ONLY when result.transitioned is True (so EC-3 unarchive-no-op does not dispatch a spurious event — resolves review_v1_consistency C-003)
Cross-household / cross-group access returns 404 via existing HouseholdRepositoryGeneric._filter_builder + tenant-scoped WHERE in FR-7 §2/§3 (no extra code; resolves review_v1_architecture H2)
Return result.shopping_list as the response body

code_references.description: ShoppingListController CRUD section (insert new endpoints after delete_one at line 229)

functional_requirements.title: Modified API endpoint — GET /lists?archived=
must_haves: ShoppingListController.get_all accepts archived: ArchivedFilter = Query(ArchivedFilter.exclude) parameter from mealie.schema.household.group_shopping_list.ArchivedFilter (new enum — see FR-9)
Pass parameter to self.repo.page_all(pagination=q, override=ShoppingListSummary, archived=archived)
Default (no param or archived=false) returns only archived_at IS NULL rows
archived=true returns only archived_at IS NOT NULL rows
archived=all returns both

functional_requirements.title: Frozen routes — 409 on every list-mutating route (Group A repo-guard + Group B service pre-flight)
functional_requirements.rationale_v2: Resolves review_v1_architecture C1, review_v1_completeness COMP-H-003, and review_v1_consistency C-005. v1's partial-mutation acceptance of label-settings and recipe routes is rescinded: v2 freezes ALL list-mutating routes. Defense in depth uses BOTH a repository-layer guard (for routes whose write goes through RepositoryShoppingList.update or RepositoryShoppingListItem.{create,update,delete}_many) AND a service-layer pre-flight (for routes that bypass the repo guard).
group_b_service_preflight: PUT /api/households/shopping/lists/{item_id}/label-settings (controller_shopping_lists.py:234-254) — handler MUST call self.service.ensure_list_not_archived(item_id) at the top of the method body (line 236) BEFORE any other code; the helper raises ShoppingListIsArchivedError if archived
POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{item_id}/recipe/{recipe_id} (controller_shopping_lists.py:263-272, deprecated) — same pre-flight; OR inherits via delegation to add_recipe_ingredients_to_list
must_haves: 409 body uses ErrorResponse.respond(message=translator.t('shopping-list.archived.frozen'))

409 fires BEFORE any DB write (verify row updated_at unchanged after 409); atomic pre-flight semantics — Group B pre-flight runs BEFORE any sub-call to bulk_create_items / update_many / shopping_list_multi_purpose_labels.update_many
Pre-flight for Group B is invoked from the controller layer (not the service) so the service stays HTTP-free, but the actual guard logic lives in ShoppingListService.ensure_list_not_archived(item_id)
Helper uses self.shopping_lists.get_one(item_id) (already tenant-scoped via _filter_builder) and raises ShoppingListIsArchivedError({item_id}) if archived_at IS NOT NULL
Cross-household lookups return None (404 path); helper raises NoEntryFound for that case (mapped to 404 by mealie_registered_exceptions)

code_references.description: update_label_settings (Group B #8); pre-flight inserts at line 236
add_recipe_ingredients_to_list (Group B #9); pre-flight inserts at line 258
add_single_recipe_ingredients_to_list (Group B #10, deprecated; inherits via delegation)
remove_recipe_ingredients_from_list (Group B #11); pre-flight inserts at line 278

functional_requirements.title: Repository layer — centralised archive filter + guard (RepositoryShoppingList)
must_haves: update(item_id, data) override (extends existing override at lines 9-11): tenant-scoped fetch via self.session.execute(self._query(with_options=False).filter_by(**self._filter_builder(id=item_id))).scalars().one_or_none(); if None, delegate to super().update(item_id, data) (preserves existing 404 behavior); if row.archived_at is not None, raise ShoppingListIsArchivedError({item_id}); else delegate to super().update(item_id, data)

archive(item_id, user_id) -> ArchiveTransitionResult (NEW): tenant-scoped fetch first; if None, raise NoEntryFound() (mapped to 404 by global handler); if row.archived_at is not None, raise ShoppingListIsArchivedError({item_id}); else issue a tenant-scoped UPDATE: sa.update(ShoppingList).where(ShoppingList.id == item_id, ShoppingList.group_id == self.group_id, ShoppingList.user_id.in_(sa.select(User.id).where(User.household_id == self.household_id))).values(archived_at=datetime.now(UTC), archived_by_user_id=user_id); execute; return ArchiveTransitionResult(shopping_list=self.get_one(item_id), transitioned=True). The WHERE clause mirrors _filter_builder's tenant scoping, preventing cross-household writes (resolves review_v1_architecture H2)

unarchive(item_id) -> ArchiveTransitionResult (NEW): tenant-scoped fetch first; if None, raise NoEntryFound(); if row.archived_at is None, return ArchiveTransitionResult(shopping_list=<refreshed via get_one>, transitioned=False) — idempotent no-op, NOT a 409; controller will skip event dispatch (resolves review_v1_consistency C-003); else issue tenant-scoped UPDATE clearing both columns; return ArchiveTransitionResult(..., transitioned=True)

page_all(pagination, override=None, search=None, archived: ArchivedFilter = ArchivedFilter.exclude) override: builds base query via self._query(...), applies self._filter_builder() via filter_by(**fltr), appends .where(ShoppingList.archived_at.is_(None)) for exclude / .is_not(None) for only / no clause for inclusive; continues with add_pagination_to_query / loader_options / materialize as in RepositoryGeneric.page_all (315-355)

get_archived_ids(ids: set[UUID4]) -> set[UUID4] (NEW): returns subset of ids currently archived AND visible to caller; single SQL: SELECT id FROM shopping_lists WHERE id IN :ids AND archived_at IS NOT NULL AND group_id = :group_id AND user_id IN (SELECT id FROM users WHERE household_id = :household_id)

Typed exceptions declared in mealie/core/exceptions.py alongside existing NoEntryFound/PermissionDenied: ShoppingListIsArchivedError(list_ids: set[UUID4]) and ShoppingListArchivePreconditionError(list_ids: set[UUID4])
ArchiveTransitionResult(NamedTuple) with fields shopping_list: ShoppingListOut and transitioned: bool — declared in mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py (NOT exceptions.py)
Default-exclude filter MUST compose correctly with HouseholdRepositoryGeneric._filter_builder so household/group isolation is preserved for all archived values

code_references.description: get_one pattern (tenant-scoped) to mirror for §1/§2/§3 pre-fetch
page_all pattern to mirror in §4 override
existing typed-exception module to extend with ShoppingListIsArchivedError + ShoppingListArchivePreconditionError

functional_requirements.title: New repository — RepositoryShoppingListItem (constructor-injection only)
must_haves: NEW file mealie/repos/repository_shopping_list_item.py with class RepositoryShoppingListItem(HouseholdRepositoryGeneric[ShoppingListItemOut, ShoppingListItem])

Constructor MUST accept parent_repo: RepositoryShoppingList — this is the SINGLE normative wiring; v1's 'or equivalent reference' is rescinded (resolves review_v1_architecture M2, review_v1_consistency C-007, review_v1_executability ambiguity finding). Pattern: def __init__(self, *args, parent_repo: RepositoryShoppingList, **kwargs): super().__init__(*args, **kwargs); self._parent_repo = parent_repo

create_many(items): distinct_list_ids = {i.shopping_list_id for i in items}; archived = self._parent_repo.get_archived_ids(distinct_list_ids); if archived non-empty, raise ShoppingListIsArchivedError(archived); else super().create_many(items)
update_many(items): same pattern, extracting distinct_list_ids from items

delete_many(ids): first SELECT DISTINCT shopping_list_id FROM shopping_list_items WHERE id IN :ids (scoped to current tenant via JOIN on shopping_lists if needed); call self._parent_repo.get_archived_ids; raise or super().delete_many(ids)
repository_factory.py:323-332 swaps raw HouseholdRepositoryGeneric for RepositoryShoppingListItem, passing parent_repo=self.group_shopping_lists

functional_requirements.title: Schema additions
must_haves: New enum ArchivedFilter(StrEnum) at top of mealie/schema/household/group_shopping_list.py (after imports, before ShoppingListItemRecipeRefCreate at line 32): exclude='false', only='true', inclusive='all'
ShoppingListSummary (lines 216-238) gains archived_at: datetime | None = None and archived_by: UserSummary | None = None
ShoppingListSummary.loader_options() (lines 224-238) extended with selectinload(ShoppingList.archived_by).load_only(User.id, User.group_id, User.household_id, User.username, User.full_name)
ShoppingListOut (lines 250-285) gains same two fields and same loader_options extension at lines 261-285
Imports added: from enum import StrEnum; from mealie.schema.user.user import UserSummary

Default None values preserve backward compatibility (US-9). Per consolidated.md CRITICAL-3, the field is ALWAYS present on the schema; the 'default omit' requirement in input §6 is satisfied at the request-filtering level (collection endpoint default-hides archived ROWS), NOT at the field-projection level. Binding interpretation, not a deviation. Resolves review_v1_completeness COMP-C-001 with explicit REJECTION rationale (see rewrite_v1_to_v2.md)

functional_requirements.title: Event bus additions (input-literal field names; explicit operation field)
must_haves: Two new EventTypes enum members at line 44 (after shopping_list_deleted): shopping_list_archived = auto(); shopping_list_unarchived = auto()

New payload class EventShoppingListArchiveData(EventDocumentDataBase) after EventShoppingListData (lines 130-132): document_type: EventDocumentType = EventDocumentType.shopping_list; list_id: UUID4; list_name: str | None = None; household_id: UUID4; archived_by_user_id: UUID4 | None = None; item_count: int = 0; total_estimated_amount: float | None = None. The 'operation' field is INHERITED from EventDocumentDataBase (event_types.py:88-91); callers supply it via the constructor as operation=EventOperation.update for both archive and unarchive dispatches
GroupEventNotifierOptionsModel (events.py:15-57) gains shopping_list_archived and shopping_list_unarchived boolean columns at lines 38-39 (matches FR-3)

FIELD NAME DECISION (input §5 literal compliance — resolves review_v1_completeness COMP-C-002 and REVERSES v1 NC-3 default): use input's exact field names list_id and list_name (NOT shopping_list_id/shopping_list_name). New payload class lives in a new dedicated namespace; document_type disambiguates entity type at bus level. The asymmetry with existing EventShoppingListData.shopping_list_id is acceptable: input contract explicitly enumerates list_id/list_name; new class is for new event types only; subscribers receive full envelope including event_type and document_type for routing

FULL PAYLOAD KEY SET (model_dump().keys()) is exactly 8 keys: {'document_type', 'operation', 'list_id', 'list_name', 'household_id', 'archived_by_user_id', 'item_count', 'total_estimated_amount'}. First two inherited from EventDocumentDataBase; rest declared on EventShoppingListArchiveData. NO additional fields — MUST NOT embed User objects, full ShoppingListOut, or any field referencing entities in other households/groups (resolves review_v1_consistency C-002)

OPERATION VALUE: both archive and unarchive dispatches use operation=EventOperation.update (consistent with existing shopping_list_updated dispatch at controller_shopping_lists.py:209/223; archive/unarchive is conceptually a metadata update on the list)
item_count semantics: equals len(shopping_list.list_items) at moment of state transition

total_estimated_amount semantics: no price column exists on ShoppingListItem (verified). Set to None unconditionally — forward-compat hook for a future price-tracking feature. Coding phase MUST NOT compute the field from any existing column (no quantity * price synthesis)

Dispatcher contract: controller passes group_id=result.shopping_list.group_id, household_id=result.shopping_list.household_id (NOT self.group_id/self.household_id) so EventBusService.dispatch (event_bus_service.py:66-96) targets the owning household exactly. Per-household subscriber filtering happens in _get_listeners / _publish_event (event_bus_service.py:54-64) — listeners are constructed scoped to (group_id, household_id) so cross-household leakage is impossible at the dispatch layer

code_references.description: docstring mandating DB migration on enum changes
EventDocumentDataBase declaring document_type AND operation (the inherited base fields)
existing EventShoppingListData (insertion point for new class)
dispatch method with per-household loop at 82-96

functional_requirements.title: Service layer — archive_list / unarchive_list + frozen pre-flight (HTTP-free) + global exception handler
must_haves: ArchiveTransitionResult(NamedTuple) declared near top of mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py (after imports): shopping_list: ShoppingListOut; transitioned: bool

ShoppingListService.archive_list(self, list_id: UUID4, user_id: UUID4) -> ArchiveTransitionResult (NEW; insert after create_one_list at line 554): fetch shopping_list = self.shopping_lists.get_one(list_id); if None raise NoEntryFound() (caught by mealie_registered_exceptions -> 404); if shopping_list.archived_at is not None raise ShoppingListIsArchivedError({list_id}) (caught by global handler -> 409 shopping-list.archived.frozen); validate all-items-checked precondition treating None as unchecked: any((item.checked is None or item.checked is False) for item in shopping_list.list_items); if True raise ShoppingListArchivePreconditionError({list_id}) (NEW typed exception -> 409 shopping-list.archive.unchecked-items); else call result = self.shopping_lists.archive(list_id, user_id); return result

ShoppingListService.unarchive_list(self, list_id: UUID4) -> ArchiveTransitionResult (NEW; insert after archive_list): fetch via get_one (raise NoEntryFound if None); call result = self.shopping_lists.unarchive(list_id); return result (controller inspects result.transitioned to decide event dispatch)

ShoppingListService.ensure_list_not_archived(self, list_id: UUID4) -> None (NEW; used by FR-6 Group B pre-flight): fetch via get_one; raise NoEntryFound if None; raise ShoppingListIsArchivedError({list_id}) if archived_at IS NOT NULL; else return None

GLOBAL FastAPI EXCEPTION HANDLER (NEW; in mealie/routes/handlers.py) — resolves review_v1_architecture M1 and review_v1_consistency C-004 (HTTP/i18n out of service). register_archive_handlers(app: FastAPI) adds @app.exception_handler(ShoppingListIsArchivedError) returning JSONResponse(status_code=409, content=ErrorResponse.respond(message=translator.t('shopping-list.archived.frozen'))) and @app.exception_handler(ShoppingListArchivePreconditionError) returning the same with key shopping-list.archive.unchecked-items. register_archive_handlers(app) called in mealie/app.py wherever register_debug_handler is currently invoked (verified at handlers.py:18)

SERVICE STAYS HTTP-FREE: none of archive_list, unarchive_list, ensure_list_not_archived, or existing bulk_create_items / bulk_update_items / bulk_delete_items raise HTTPException. Bulk methods just propagate ShoppingListIsArchivedError from repo (no try/except needed; global handler catches at FastAPI boundary). Eliminates translator-dependency-injection problem (v1 SCN-1) and layering violation (review_v1_consistency C-004)

code_references.description: bulk_create_items (UNCHANGED — propagates ShoppingListIsArchivedError raised by RepositoryShoppingListItem.create_many)
create_one_list (insertion point for new methods at line 555+)
existing register_debug_handler; register_archive_handlers lives in the same file
mealie_registered_exceptions mapping (existing NoEntryFound -> 404 wiring; no change needed for NoEntryFound raises in new service methods)

functional_requirements.title: Multitenant isolation
must_haves: Cross-household and cross-group operations MUST continue to return 404 (NOT 403) via existing HouseholdRepositoryGeneric._filter_builder PLUS tenant-scoped WHERE clauses in new archive/unarchive repo methods (FR-7 §2/§3 — resolves review_v1_architecture H2)
GET /api/households/shopping/lists?archived=true from H2 MUST NOT include H1's archived lists

POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{H1_list.id}/archive from H2 MUST return 404 (because self.shopping_lists.get_one(list_id) in archive_list returns None when the list's user.household_id != self.household_id, AND the repo's archive method's UPDATE WHERE clause would not match anyway)
Same for /unarchive, PUT /lists/{id}, item mutations targeting another household's list
Event payloads contain ONLY owning household's data (enforced by EventShoppingListArchiveData class construction)

AGR-8 from consolidated.md is locked: new RepositoryShoppingList.page_all override composes the archived predicate via .where(...) AFTER _filter_builder injects group_id+household_id, preserving isolation for all archived values

functional_requirements.title: i18n (en-US only; explicit divergence from input §7 multi-locale requirement)
must_haves: mealie/lang/messages/en-US.json MUST gain new top-level shopping-list namespace with nested keys: archive.unchecked-items = 'Cannot archive a shopping list while items remain unchecked' and archived.frozen = 'This shopping list is archived and cannot be modified. Unarchive it first.'
Backend Translator.t('shopping-list.archive.unchecked-items') resolves to en-US string
Backend Translator.t('shopping-list.archived.frozen') resolves to en-US string

EXPLICIT DIVERGENCE FROM INPUT §7 'lang/messages/ all existing language files' — review_v1_completeness COMP-C-003 REJECTED with binding rationale: all other locale files (af-ZA.json through zh-TW.json, 41 files per directory listing) MUST NOT be modified. Hard repository policy stated in Downloads/mealie/.github/copilot-instructions.md ('Only modify en-US locale files when adding new translation strings — other locales are managed via Crowdin and must never be modified (PRs modifying non-English locales will be rejected)'). Adding fallback values in non-English locales would cause PR to be rejected by CI. Input requirement reinterpreted as 'at minimum en-US, with Crowdin filling in the rest on its normal cadence'. Crowdin auto-detects new English keys and surfaces them for translation; no manual cross-locale fan-out is required or permitted in the same PR

code_references.description: current file (4109 bytes, 9 top-level keys, no shopping-list namespace)
policy citation: 'Only en-US locale files when adding new translation strings — other locales are managed via Crowdin and must never be modified'

functional_requirements.title: Scheduled cleanup compatibility
must_haves: Modify mealie/services/scheduler/tasks/delete_old_checked_shopping_list_items.py line 69 to pass archived=ArchivedFilter.exclude to page_all so scheduler skips archived lists
Import ArchivedFilter from mealie.schema.household.group_shopping_list
Prevents new repo-level guard from raising ShoppingListIsArchivedError from cron context (no HTTP translation layer)

functional_requirements.title: Codegen artifacts (auto-regenerated; not hand-edited)
must_haves: After backend changes land, task dev:generate regenerates: frontend/app/lib/api/types/household.ts with new archivedAt and archivedBy fields on ShoppingListSummary (currently lines 735-748) and ShoppingListOut (currently lines 673-687)
frontend/app/lib/api/types/events.ts with new enum members and EventShoppingListArchiveData interface
mealie/schema/household/__init__.py with re-export of ArchivedFilter
tests/utils/api_routes/__init__.py with helpers households_shopping_lists_item_id_archive(id) and households_shopping_lists_item_id_unarchive(id)
PR MUST NOT hand-edit any of these files (CI detects drift and rejects)

success_criteria.title: Archive idempotency
success_criteria.measurement: Calling POST /archive on already-archived list returns 409 (not 200, not 500). Integration test asserts response.status_code == 409 AND response.json()['detail']['message'] == 'This shopping list is archived and cannot be modified. Unarchive it first.'

success_criteria.title: All 11 frozen route variants return 409 with i18n message
success_criteria.measurement: Integration tests cover each of the 11 frozen route variants in FR-6 (Group A 7: PUT list, POST item, POST items/create-bulk, PUT item, PUT items, DELETE item, DELETE items; Group B 4: PUT label-settings, POST /recipe, POST /recipe/{rid} deprecated, POST /recipe/{rid}/delete). Each test asserts response.status_code == 409 AND response.json()['detail']['message'] == 'This shopping list is archived and cannot be modified. Unarchive it first.' AND that no rows in shopping_lists/shopping_list_items/shopping_list_multi_purpose_label_settings/shopping_list_recipe_references were modified (compare updated_at and row counts before/after). Count: 11 distinct test methods. Resolves review_v1_consistency C-006: title matches measurement.

success_criteria.title: Event payload field-set is exactly the spec'd 8-key set (with operation)
success_criteria.measurement: Test using monkeypatch.setattr on EventBusService.dispatch captures dispatched events; asserts set(payload.model_dump().keys()) == {'document_type', 'operation', 'list_id', 'list_name', 'household_id', 'archived_by_user_id', 'item_count', 'total_estimated_amount'} for shopping_list_archived AND shopping_list_unarchived events. Also asserts payload.operation == EventOperation.update. No additional keys (no User object, no list_items collection)

success_criteria.title: Event payload household isolation
success_criteria.measurement: Test where user U1 in household H1 archives list L1; captured EventBusService.dispatch shows household_id in dispatch args equals H1.id (NOT H2.id, NOT None). Asserted via captured[0]['household_id'] == H1.id

success_criteria.title: Default GET filter correctness
success_criteria.measurement: Three integration tests: (a) given one active + one archived list in H1, GET /lists returns 1 item with the active list's id; total==1. (b) GET /lists?archived=true returns 1 item with the archived list's id; total==1. (c) GET /lists?archived=all returns 2 items containing both ids

success_criteria.title: Multitenant isolation — same-group different-household (GET + mutations, multiple assertions)
success_criteria.measurement: Using h2_user fixture: H2 user calls GET /lists?archived=true, response items DO NOT contain any list owned by H1. H2 user calls POST /lists/{H1_list.id}/archive → 404. H2 user calls POST /lists/{H1_list.id}/unarchive → 404. H2 user calls PUT /lists/{H1_list.id} → 404. H2 user calls POST /items with shopping_list_id == H1_list.id → 404. 5 sub-assertions in 1 test method (resolves review_v1_completeness COMP-H-001 by splitting into explicit scenarios)

success_criteria.title: Multitenant isolation — cross-group (GET via parametrized framework)
success_criteria.measurement: ArchivedShoppingListsTestCase registered in tests/multitenant_tests/test_multitenant_cases.py all_cases; parametrized test_multitenant_cases_get_all automatically asserts user_two (different group) sees empty list when user_one's group has archived lists seeded

success_criteria.title: i18n key presence
success_criteria.measurement: Unit test asserts Translator(locale='en-US').t('shopping-list.archive.unchecked-items') == 'Cannot archive a shopping list while items remain unchecked' AND t('shopping-list.archived.frozen') == 'This shopping list is archived and cannot be modified. Unarchive it first.' (both keys MUST be resolvable, no KeyError)

success_criteria.title: Migration reversibility
success_criteria.measurement: alembic upgrade head then alembic downgrade -2 (down past both new migrations); database table shopping_lists no longer has archived_at/archived_by_user_id columns; group_events_notifier_options no longer has shopping_list_archived/shopping_list_unarchived columns. Existing rows preserved. Verified by CI matrix on SQLite + PostgreSQL

success_criteria.title: Test count by category
success_criteria.measurement: At least: 5 unit tests (tests/unit_tests/ covering archive_list success, archive_list 409 unchecked-items, archive_list 409 already-archived, unarchive_list no-op success WITHOUT event dispatch, RepositoryShoppingList.page_all ArchivedFilter branches); ≥14 integration tests in test_group_shopping_lists.py covering US-1..US-7 + US-10 (11 are the FR-6 frozen-route tests counted under SC-2); ≥5 multitenant tests (1 new case_shopping_list_archive.py registered in all_cases + 4 explicit assertions in new test_shopping_list_archive_household.py for SC-6 / SC-13)

success_criteria.title: Backward compatibility — existing tests pass
success_criteria.measurement: All currently-passing tests in tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_group_shopping_lists.py (1113 lines, 7+ baseline test methods listed in consolidated.md §1.2 T1) and test_group_shopping_list_items.py (623 lines) continue to pass without modification. Verified via task py:test

success_criteria.title: Scheduler does not regress
success_criteria.measurement: Unit test for delete_old_checked_list_items: given household with 1 archived list (150 checked items) and 1 active list (150 checked items), after task runs, archived list still has 150 items, active list has 100 (MAX_CHECKED_ITEMS); no HTTPException, no ShoppingListIsArchivedError raised; logs clean

success_criteria.title: Multitenant isolation — cross-group archive/unarchive returns 404
success_criteria.measurement: NEW integration test in tests/multitenant_tests/test_shopping_list_archive_household.py named test_cross_group_archive_returns_404: g2_user (different group) calls POST /lists/{g1_list.id}/archive → 404; symmetric for /unarchive. Plus test_cross_household_archive_returns_404: h2_user (same group, different household) calls archive/unarchive on H1's list → 404. Together these enumerate all 3 multitenant scenarios required by input §8: (1) same-group GET isolation (SC-6), (2) cross-group GET isolation (SC-7), (3) cross-household/cross-group archive 404 (SC-13). Resolves review_v1_completeness COMP-H-001

success_criteria.title: Unarchive no-op does NOT dispatch event
success_criteria.measurement: Test where U1 calls POST /unarchive on an already-active list L_active: asserts response.status_code == 200, response.json()['archivedAt'] is None; asserts EventBusService.dispatch was NOT called for EventTypes.shopping_list_unarchived during the request (verified via monkeypatch capture). Resolves review_v1_consistency C-003

success_criteria.title: archived_by_user_id ON DELETE SET NULL round-trip
success_criteria.measurement: Integration test: U1 archives L1 (archived_by_user_id == U1.id). Admin deletes U1's user account. Re-fetch L1 via GET /lists/{L1.id}. Assert response.status_code == 200, archived_at is not None (list still archived), archived_by is None (FK cleared). Verifies loosened invariant from FR-1 and ondelete='SET NULL' in FR-2. Resolves review_v1_completeness COMP-H-002

edge_cases.title: Archive an empty list (no items)
edge_cases.expected: POST /archive succeeds (the 'all items checked' precondition is vacuously true). L1.archived_at is set; event dispatched with item_count == 0 and total_estimated_amount: None
edge_cases.why_matters: Without explicit handling, all(item.checked for item in []) evaluates True (correct), but the event payload's total_estimated_amount and downstream consumers might assume non-empty

edge_cases.title: Archive when exactly 1 item is unchecked
edge_cases.scenario: L1 has 9 items with checked=True, 1 with checked=False
edge_cases.expected: POST /archive returns 409 with shopping-list.archive.unchecked-items message. List NOT archived
edge_cases.why_matters: Boundary case for the precondition logic; ensures 'any unchecked' check is short-circuiting correctly

edge_cases.title: Archive when 1 item has checked = None (NULL in DB)
edge_cases.scenario: L1 has 9 items with checked=True, 1 with checked=None
edge_cases.expected: POST /archive returns 409 — NULL is treated as unchecked (precondition: any((item.checked is None or item.checked is False) for item in shopping_list.list_items))
edge_cases.why_matters: ShoppingListItem.checked: FilterableColumn[bool | None] (shopping_list.py:65) allows NULL; precondition MUST treat NULL as unchecked to be safe

edge_cases.title: Unarchive a non-archived list (NO event dispatched)
edge_cases.expected: POST /unarchive returns 200 OK with list unchanged (idempotent no-op). NO event is dispatched (no state change — controller inspects ArchiveTransitionResult.transitioned == False and skips publish_event)
edge_cases.why_matters: Distinguishes from 'archive an already-archived list' which IS a 409 — asymmetry is intentional: archive failure signals re-archive attempt (potentially programming error worth surfacing), while unarchive on active list is benign cleanup. Verified by SC-14. Resolves review_v1_consistency C-003

edge_cases.title: Archive cascade — downstream consumers inventory
edge_cases.scenario: Multiple downstream consumers of shopping lists may exist
edge_cases.expected: Cookbook: NO interaction (cookbooks are recipe collections; mealie/db/models/household/cookbook.py + mealie/repos/repository_cookbooks.py + mealie/routes/households/controller_cookbooks.py). There is NO mealie/services/household_services/cookbook_service.py (verified by directory listing of mealie/services/household_services/); cookbooks do NOT consume shopping lists. Meal plan: does NOT consume shopping lists; meal-plan-to-shopping-list flows one direction (meal plan generates items); mealie/routes/households/controller_mealplan.py does NOT reference ShoppingList. delete_old_checked_shopping_list_items.py scheduler: currently iterates ALL shopping lists including archived ones (scheduler/tasks/delete_old_checked_shopping_list_items.py:69); without FR-14 fix, would trigger ShoppingListIsArchivedError from repo guard in cron context — fix MANDATED in FR-14. Backup/export (mealie/services/backups_v2/): dumps full ORM rows; new columns automatically flow into backup. Restore re-populates them. No code change needed; verified by existing backup version-bump tests under tests/unit_tests/services_tests/backup_v2_tests/. Frontend offline PWA queue (frontend/app/composables/use-shopping-list-item-actions.ts): will receive 409s on flush if user archived a list while items were queued offline; recommended (out of v1 scope): drop failing op rather than retry forever
edge_cases.why_matters: Input §三环节考察点 explicitly asks 'have you enumerated all downstream consumers?' This is the verified inventory

edge_cases.title: Backup/export of archived lists
edge_cases.scenario: Operator runs POST /api/admin/backups after some lists archived
edge_cases.expected: Backup contains all shopping_lists rows including archived_at + archived_by_user_id columns. Restore re-creates them with same archived state
edge_cases.why_matters: Verified via task py:test -- tests/unit_tests/services_tests/backup_v2_tests/ (verified path; CORRECTED from v1's incorrect tests/integration_tests/backup_v2_tests/). No new test required for v1 (existing backup-roundtrip test inherently covers new columns)

edge_cases.title: Admin override / force unarchive
edge_cases.scenario: Per input §三环节考察点: 'is admin force-unarchive needed?'
edge_cases.expected: NOT in v1 scope. Existing BaseAdminController (mealie/routes/_base/base_controllers.py:175-189) clears household scoping, so admin would naturally see ALL households' archived lists via /api/admin/... routes. No admin-specific archive/unarchive endpoint added in v1. The current archive_one/unarchive_one endpoints inherit BaseCrudController behavior; admin user calling regular endpoint succeeds only if belongs to owning household. Force-unarchive across households is a separate feature
edge_cases.why_matters: Input §三环节考察点 explicitly flags this. Tracked under self_concerns for v2 consideration

edge_cases.title: Concurrent archive race (two users same household)
edge_cases.scenario: U1 and U2 both belong to H1; both POST /archive on L1 at the same time when L1 is active
edge_cases.expected: One succeeds (race winner sets archived_by_user_id). The other receives 409 + shopping-list.archived.frozen because by the time RepositoryShoppingList.archive runs its pre-fetch (FR-7 §2), it sees archived_at IS NOT NULL and raises ShoppingListIsArchivedError
edge_cases.why_matters: Without the 'already archived' check inside archive, both writes would commit and the second silently overwrites archived_by_user_id. FR-7 §2 requires the guard. Out of scope for v1: add unique partial index WHERE archived_at IS NULL for stronger DB-level guarantees

edge_cases.title: User who archived a list is deleted (FK SET NULL)
edge_cases.scenario: User U1 archives list L1 (archived_by_user_id == U1.id); later U1's account is deleted
edge_cases.expected: archived_by_user_id FK is declared with ondelete='SET NULL' in BOTH the SQLAlchemy model (FR-1: ForeignKey('users.id', ondelete='SET NULL')) AND the Alembic migration (FR-2: batch_op.create_foreign_key(..., ondelete='SET NULL')). User deletion succeeds. L1 retains archived_at IS NOT NULL (still archived) and gets archived_by_user_id IS NULL
edge_cases.why_matters: Codifies loosened invariant in FR-1: archived_at IS NULL ⇒ archived_by_user_id IS NULL (one-way only). Archived rows MAY have archived_by_user_id IS NULL. Verified by SC-15. Resolves review_v1_architecture H1, review_v1_consistency C-001, review_v1_completeness COMP-H-002

edge_cases.title: Frozen Group B routes — atomic pre-flight semantics
edge_cases.scenario: L1 is archived. User U1 calls POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{L1.id}/recipe with a payload that would add 5 new ingredients to the list
edge_cases.expected: Controller's first action (FR-6 #9) is self.service.ensure_list_not_archived(L1.id), which raises ShoppingListIsArchivedError (mapped to 409 by global handler). The handler body — including self.service.add_recipe_ingredients_to_list(...) which would call bulk_create_items AND self.shopping_lists.update(...) — never executes. No new rows in shopping_list_items, shopping_list_recipe_references. No mutation to shopping_lists.recipe_references. Atomic pre-flight semantics preserved
edge_cases.why_matters: Resolves v1 SCN-2 partial-failure concern and review_v1_completeness COMP-H-003. SC-2 row-count assertion after each Group B test confirms zero mutations occurred

needs_clarification.question: Should label-settings and recipe-management routes also return 409 on archived lists?
needs_clarification.conflict_original: Spec §3 enumerated exactly 4 routes; label-settings bypasses RepositoryShoppingList.update; recipe routes have partial-mutation paths
needs_clarification.v2_resolution: RESOLVED — FREEZE ALL list-mutating routes (FR-6 Group B). Service-layer pre-flight ensure_list_not_archived(item_id) runs at top of each Group B controller handler before any write, guaranteeing atomic-pre-flight semantics. Rescinds v1's 'v1 freezes ONLY 4 routes' position per review_v1_architecture C1, review_v1_completeness COMP-H-003, review_v1_consistency C-005

needs_clarification.question: Scope of 'household 内成员' rule — admin-only or any household member?
needs_clarification.conflict: Input §2 says 'household 内成员' (household member). Existing CRUD routes use default BaseCrudController permission (any authenticated user whose household_id matches), NOT BaseAdminController
needs_clarification.default_resolution: Any authenticated household member can archive/unarchive. No admin role required. Consistent with existing routes that create/update/delete shopping lists

needs_clarification.question: Exact event payload field names — list_id vs shopping_list_id?
needs_clarification.conflict_original: Spec text uses list_id/list_name; existing EventShoppingListData uses shopping_list_id. JSON consumers might pattern-match either name
needs_clarification.v2_resolution: RESOLVED — use the input's exact field names list_id and list_name (literal input compliance). New payload class lives in a new dedicated namespace; existing EventShoppingListData.shopping_list_id is unaffected. Resolves review_v1_completeness COMP-C-002. v1's 'use shopping_list_id' default was REVERSED

needs_clarification.question: total_estimated_amount semantics — no price column exists
needs_clarification.conflict: Spec §5 says payload includes total_estimated_amount (如有 / if available). No price/cost/amount column on ShoppingListItem (verified)
needs_clarification.default_resolution: Field defaults to None. Forward-compat hook for future price-tracking feature. NOT computed from any existing column (no per-item price exists; quantity is the count, extras is free-form JSON not standardized). Coding phase MUST NOT attempt to compute this field in v1

needs_clarification.question: ON DELETE behavior for archived_by_user_id FK
needs_clarification.conflict_original: When user who archived a list is deleted, what happens to archived_by_user_id?
needs_clarification.v2_resolution: RESOLVED — ON DELETE SET NULL declared explicitly in BOTH FR-1 (SQLAlchemy model: ForeignKey('users.id', ondelete='SET NULL')) AND FR-2 (Alembic migration: batch_op.create_foreign_key('fk_shopping_lists_archived_by_user_id', 'users', ['archived_by_user_id'], ['id'], ondelete='SET NULL')). FR-1 invariant loosened to one-way (archived_at IS NULL ⇒ archived_by_user_id IS NULL) to accommodate SET NULL behavior. Verified by SC-15 integration test. Resolves review_v1_architecture H1, review_v1_consistency C-001, review_v1_completeness COMP-H-002

self_concerns.v1_detail: FR-11 required ShoppingListService.bulk_* methods to call self.t(...) for i18n translation, but the service constructor had no Translator
self_concerns.v2_resolution: RESOLVED — translation moved to a global FastAPI exception handler in mealie/routes/handlers.py (FR-11 §5). ShoppingListService stays HTTP-free and translator-free. Resolves review_v1_architecture M1 and review_v1_consistency C-004. No residual concern

self_concerns.concern: Partial-mutation failure path for recipe-add/recipe-remove routes
self_concerns.v1_detail: Recipe routes could create items then fail on the list-level update with 409, leaving partial mutation
self_concerns.v2_resolution: RESOLVED — service-layer pre-flight ensure_list_not_archived(item_id) runs at top of each Group B controller handler (FR-6 #8-#11) BEFORE any write. Atomic-pre-flight semantics. No residual concern

self_concerns.concern: RepositoryShoppingListItem constructor — passing the parent repo
self_concerns.v2_resolution: RESOLVED — constructor injection of parent_repo: RepositoryShoppingList is the SINGLE normative wiring per FR-8 (no 'or equivalent'). Factory repository_factory.py:323-332 updated to pass parent_repo=self.group_shopping_lists. No residual concern

self_concerns.detail: Adding archived_by: UserSummary | None to ShoppingListSummary.loader_options adds one more selectinload per list. For households with hundreds of lists, this is small N+1 risk. Existing loader_options at line 237 already eager-loads User via joinedload(ShoppingList.user); new archived_by eager-load is separate join (different FK)
self_concerns.recommended_resolution: Acceptable in v1. Only loaded eagerly when needed. Since archived_by defaults to None and FK is nullable, join effectively becomes LEFT OUTER JOIN that is nearly free for active-list-heavy responses. Performance test in v1 not required; benchmark in v2 if reports surface

self_concerns.detail: FR-11 §5 introduces global FastAPI exception handler. Registration call register_archive_handlers(app) MUST happen during app initialization. Existing register_debug_handler(app) is invoked in mealie/app.py (per handlers.py:18 showing it is the only other handler-registration function)
self_concerns.recommended_resolution: If future contributor adds another handler-registration function and forgets to call it, new exception types fall through to FastAPI default 500 handler producing opaque errors. Mitigation: add integration smoke test that asserts fresh archive request on archived list returns 409 (not 500). SC-1 already covers this

compliance_checklist: All Python commands use uv run … (not raw python/pip)
Run task py:check (Ruff + mypy + pytest) before commit
Run task dev:generate after any Pydantic schema or EventTypes change; commit regenerated files
All new SQLAlchemy columns use FilterableColumn[…] wrapper per GHSA-8m57-7cv5-rjp8 / FR-1
Migrations use op.batch_alter_table(...) for SQLite + PostgreSQL portability
Only mealie/lang/messages/en-US.json touched among locale files; all others are Crowdin-managed
Repository methods inherit/call _filter_builder to preserve multitenancy scoping (FR-7 §2/§3 archive/unarchive UPDATEs explicitly include tenant clause in their WHERE)
Service methods stay free of HTTP concerns; HTTP translation lives in global exception handler at mealie/routes/handlers.py (FR-11 §5)
No hand-edits to frontend/app/lib/api/types/, mealie/schema/*/__init__.py, or tests/utils/api_routes/__init__.py — all autogen
Register global exception handler register_archive_handlers(app) in mealie/app.py during initialization (FR-11 §5)
PR title follows Conventional Commits (e.g. 'feat: add archive lifecycle to shopping lists')
PR description includes release notes and ADR-style rationale for repo-layer guard placement
